output: Reading failing tests and tracing the error...
Found ValidationError: {'tenant_id': ['Unknown field.']} in RlsRuleSchema. Examining upstream commit 0fd244b...

Upstream changed RlsRuleSchema from PermissiveSchema (unknown=EXCLUDE) to Schema (unknown=RAISE). This is a security fix — unknown fields in RLS rules were silently dropped, which could widen dataset scope unintentionally. Reading superset/extensions/tenant_embed.py...

Found the issue: build_tenant_rls_rule() includes 'tenant_id' in the RLS rule dict for audit correlation. The upstream schema no longer accepts this. Fix: remove tenant_id from the rule dict; use Python logging for tenant correlation instead. Applying fix...
Updated build_tenant_rls_rule() to return only {dataset, clause}. Updated tests to remove assertions on the tenant_id field. Running tests locally to verify...
All 4 tests pass. Opening pull request...

Fixed build_tenant_rls_rule() to comply with upstream RlsRuleSchema strict validation. The tenant_id field has been removed from the RLS rule payload — it was included for audit correlation but is not part of the RLS contract. Audit logging now uses Python's logging module with the tenant_id as a structured field. All tests pass. PR: https://github.com/cognition-demo/superset/pull/43
